behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: PR-changed-file collection must feed the analyzer correctly. MOCK-MISS: this is not just one pure function; bug appears when collector/analyzer wiring is wrong (e.g., path normalization mismatch). REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only says changed-vs-unchanged selection, not real HTTP/GitHub runtime conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the changed-file provider module and analysis coordinator together with a fixture PR diff and mixed repository tree; assert analyzer is invoked only for paths in the PR change set and never for unchanged paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans analyzer-output aggregation plus comment rendering/post payload assembly. MOCK-MISS: grouping bugs can occur at boundary between findings schema and comment formatter. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): no explicit need for real network/OS; posting target can be stubbed while validating produced comment body. (b)(c)(d): no AI/ML or agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text focuses on grouping content in the comment, not real GitHub API delivery semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run findings aggregator + PR comment composer together on mixed-severity findings; assert generated comment contains distinct error/warning/info sections with correct membership and counts before API client send.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA boundary is explicit: GitHub Actions timeout and end-to-end runtime under CI conditions. In-process fakes can hide subprocess startup, filesystem throughput, and runner-level constraints (MOCK-HIDE). No LLM dependency. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires meeting the '10-minute Actions timeout', a real CI runtime condition.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step as it runs in CI (same entrypoint/process model) against a generated 200-file change set in a runner-like environment; assert wall-clock completion under 10 minutes including analysis and PR-comment generation path.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly says 'linter process crashes', so failure boundary is subprocess crash handling and recovery. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): requires real process exit/crash semantics; exception stubs may miss signal/exit-code behavior and stream truncation (MOCK-HIDE). No AI/ML or multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because concrete text requires process-crash resilience, not just in-process error propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with real linter subprocess over a batch where one malformed file causes the subprocess to crash; verify supervisor records failure, continues analyzing remaining files (via subsequent process invocations or isolated tasks), and publishes consolidated results.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is deterministic dedup logic on finding keys (file/line/rule overlap), which is a local algorithmic boundary. MOCK-MISS: no required cross-module contract in behavior text. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML, no multi-step agentic flow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because text specifies output dedup rule semantics, not module handoff or infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call dedup function with a findings list containing intentional same-file same-line overlaps from different rule passes; assert exactly one output entry per dedup key and non-duplicates remain untouched.
